title: Standard Type Plane Standard
summary: Defines the standards-first navigation type plane: broad artifact types route first to governing standards, and projections such as the Kind Atlas, System Atlas, paper-module sidecars, and option surfaces read from those standards.
purpose: Make standards the landing authority for both writing and navigating artifact types. A standard says how a file is written, how that artifact kind is read, what source authority owns it, which projections expose it, how currentness is checked, and which validation proves it.
authority_posture: Authoritative contract. This standard governs the type-plane shape; generated type-plane databases, atlas rows, option-surface rows, and paper-module sidecars are projections and must route mutation back to the owning standard.

operator_pressure.statement: The standards layer must be the place where navigation and write contracts land. Paper modules and atlas surfaces are useful projections, but the durable rule belongs in standards.

core_rule: Navigation begins with artifact type. Artifact type points to a governing standard. The governing standard declares source authority, write lane, read bands, option surface, atlas projection, currentness policy, evidence drilldown, and validation probe. Projections may browse or query this shape; they do not own it.

cluster_flag.purpose: Cluster standards type-plane rows by row status so agents can choose an artifact family before opening type rows.

flag.purpose: Expose one standards type-plane row with governing standards, source authority, option surface, projection refs, currentness, validation, and gap summary.

card.purpose: Bind a selected type-plane row to its standard authority, read/write contract, projection boundary, renderer passport, gap row, and validation route.
forbidden: raw receipts or private operator state

band:card.source: selected type-plane node, generated gap rows, renderer passport projection, and source type_plane_rows entry

source_authority.projection_boundary: The navigation_type_plane option surface is a generated standards-first browse projection. Source truth stays in std_standard_type_plane and the standards each type row names.

currentness_policy.rule: After changes to std_standard_type_plane rows or renderer contracts, check the generated graph/index/gap/renderer projections before relying on navigation_type_plane cards.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Card rows expose first-order governing standards, generated projection refs, renderer passport refs, known gaps, and validation probes. Wider atlas, paper-module, annex, and Task Ledger neighborhoods downshift to counts and drilldown commands.

type_plane_rows.title: Standards
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/standards/**/*.json plus family authority indexes
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Open standards_registry.json and the owning group authority index before creating or revising std_*.json.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the standards option surface to select a standard, then open the JSON authority.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project standards as Standard entities with governed_by and validates edges.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Validate JSON and refresh standards option surfaces through their existing builder/read path.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: apply_patch or governing builder; generated projections are never hand-edited.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex logic may create or revise a standard only after std_annex_distillation rows name source_locus, local target, adoption lane, and validation.
known_gaps: System Atlas ingestion of type-plane rows remains owned by cap_atlas_standard_type_plane_ingestion.

type_plane_rows.title: Standards Compliance Projection

compression_passport.atom: Type-plane bridge from standard quality questions into scanner coverage, compliance findings, generated ledger rows, and navigation gap repair routes.
compression_passport.when_to_open: Open when a task asks whether standards are compliant, which standards have scanner coverage, how Atlas/navigation should consume compliance state, or which standard gaps are actionable.
compression_passport.when_not_to_open: Do not open as a substitute for the governing standard source, a mutation authority for generated ledger state, or a claim that every baseline-only standard has a domain scanner.
landmines: Compliance ledger rows are generated projections, not source authority.
Baseline inventory coverage is discoverability, not proof of domain-specific compliance.
Finding repair belongs in the owning standard, adapter, builder, or Task Ledger cap.
sufficiency_claims: Routes type questions through std_compliance_coverage and std_compliance_finding before opening ledger details.
Names the scanner registry, generated ledger, option-surface command, validation probes, and known Atlas/navigation gap.

type_plane_rows.source_authority: system/lib/compliance adapters plus codex/standards/std_compliance_coverage.json and codex/standards/std_compliance_finding.json; codex/hologram/compliance/ledger.json is the generated read model and must not be hand-edited.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Add or revise domain scanners under system/lib/compliance, register them in system/lib/compliance/__init__.py, and keep findings shaped by std_compliance_finding. Rebuild the compliance ledger through tools/meta/factory/build_compliance_ledger.py after scanner or standard changes.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Start with compliance_ledger cluster_flag, drill to affected standard cards, then use navigation_type_plane gaps or the governing standard card to choose the owner lane before opening source files.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project standards compliance as generated quality state with scanned_by, reports_finding, and repairs_via edges, but mutation routes back to the governing standard, scanner adapter, builder, or Task Ledger cap.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Current only when the compliance ledger projection, navigation type-plane projection, and renderer passports match a fresh build from std_standard_type_plane and registered compliance adapters.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Mutate the relevant std_*.json, compliance adapter, scanner registry, or builder; then rerun the compliance ledger, navigation type-plane, and renderer passport builders. Never hand-edit codex/hologram/compliance/ledger.json or state/navigation_type_plane/*.json.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: External architecture-health or compliance-scanning patterns may motivate new scanner checks only after they name the local standard, artifact class, candidate finding shape, and validation probe.
known_gaps: System Atlas explicit card drilldowns can project standards_compliance_projection rows from std_standard_type_plane when generated graph rows are stale or absent; generated graph-edge ingestion remains part of cap_atlas_standard_type_plane_ingestion.
Many standards intentionally remain baseline_inventory_only until a domain scanner is worth the maintenance cost.

type_plane_rows.title: Principles
type_plane_rows.source_authority: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Use principles curation: complete pri_* rows with evidence, tests, edges, failure modes, and decision examples.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Cluster by native principle kind, then card or tape selected ids.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Principle entities and governed_by edges to std_raw_seed_principles.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Principles are curated; evidence refs must be reopened for promotion or reversal-sensitive work.
validation_probe: jq '.' 'obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json'
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Direct governed hand-mint or raw_seed_apply_loop.py hand-mint-principle; do not hand-edit raw_seed.md.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annexes can corroborate or motivate principles only through local curation, never by making external text the principle authority.
known_gaps: Many principle rows still lack teleology profiles; cap_principle_teleology_navigation_population owns this population work.

type_plane_rows.title: Concepts
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/concepts/con_*.json
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Concept rows are curated abstract theory with principle and mechanism edges.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use concept option surfaces and card rows before opening concept JSON bodies.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Concept entities and edges to principles, mechanisms, and standards.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Concepts survive code refactors; edge and evidence checks matter more than code mtimes.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Follow std_concept and doctrine concept curation; refine existing concepts before duplicates.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Lane 2 annex pattern transfer may create a concept only when the pattern is abstract theory and carries structured annex provenance.

type_plane_rows.title: Mechanisms
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/mechanisms/mech_*.json
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Mechanisms require code_loci or explicit implementation_gap; unimplemented runbooks belong elsewhere.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use mechanism option surfaces and card rows to inspect code-grounded patterns.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Mechanism entities with implements, validates, reads, and writes edges.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Mechanisms are drift-sensitive and must be reviewed when code_loci change.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Follow std_mechanism and mechanism_authoring_reflex.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex logic may produce or refine a mechanism only when local code embodies or will embody the pattern with code_loci/proof.

type_plane_rows.title: Paper Modules
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/paper_modules/*.md for authored subsystem projections; generated sidecars for browse/coverage only.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Paper modules describe bounded subsystem projections under std_paper_module. They do not become landing authority for standards-grade rules.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use paper-module option surfaces, paper-module cards, route coverage, and validation report.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project PaperModule entities and covered_by_paper_module edges, with mutation routed back to std_paper_module or the authored module as appropriate.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Freshness comes from build_paper_module_index.py source manifests and route fingerprints.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit authored module markdown only; refresh sidecars through the builder and avoid broad sidecar churn from unrelated dirty modules.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex patterns may link to paper modules as local targets, but standards-grade navigation rules must land in standards first.
known_gaps: Global paper-module validation has existing legacy debt; cap_paper_module_standard_compliance_projection_cleanup owns cleanup and scoping.

type_plane_rows.title: Annex Distillation Patterns
type_plane_rows.source_authority: annexes/<slug>/distillation.json plus source_locus paths inside annexes/<slug>/repo/
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Rows name exact pattern logic, adoption lane, local target, source_locus, status, and validation/currentness posture.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use annex inspiration, annex dogfood, distillation index, and exact pattern cards before adopting.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Annex and external_prior_art edges, but local authority begins only after adoption into local standards/doctrine/code.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Annex currentness, route coverage, and distillation status must be checked before reuse.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Per-annex distillation rows are authority; aggregate indexes are generated.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: This is the source pattern type for annex use. Adoption must land in a local target standard, principle, concept, mechanism, skill, builder, or code path with provenance.
known_gaps: cap_annex_currentness_navigation_ratchet owns currentness feedback and repair ratchets.

type_plane_rows.title: System Atlas
type_plane_rows.source_authority: state/system_atlas/system_atlas.graph.json as generated projection; source files/standards/ledgers remain authority.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Do not hand-edit atlas state; refresh through build_system_atlas.py.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use atlas cluster/card/unknowns bands as drilldown after entry selection.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: The atlas is the projection consumer of this type plane, not the source of it.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Atlas rows carry freshness_status and source manifests.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Refresh builder only.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Atlas may expose annex-derived edges only as external prior art or adopted local artifact provenance.
known_gaps: cap_atlas_standard_type_plane_ingestion owns ingestion of type-plane rows.

type_plane_rows.title: Skills
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/skills/skill_registry.json plus codex/doctrine/skills/<family>/<skill>.md per-skill bodies
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author a complete skill row (id, kind, family, status, triggers, description, doctrine_edges, agent_surface) in skill_registry.json paired with a markdown body; pick a skill_type from std_skill_types.json. Doctrine edges name the principles, concepts, and mechanisms the skill instantiates.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the skills option surface (cluster_flag, then flag, then card) and the registry as authority; load the markdown body only after a stable skill row is selected. Skills are not principles or paper modules; they are repeatable agent moves with triggers and outputs.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Skill entities with doctrine_edges (concepts, mechanisms, principles), family edges, and trigger groupings. Mutation routes back to skill_registry.json plus the markdown body, never atlas state.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Skills are drift-sensitive: agent_surface fields, kernel_commands, and focus_paths must track real CLI flags and source paths. Freshness rides on build_skill_catalog_projection.py and is consumed by AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CODEX.md, and skill_map.md generated regions.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit codex/doctrine/skills/skill_registry.json and the matching skill markdown together; refresh projection through build_skill_catalog_projection.py. Never hand-edit skill_map.md, AGENTS.md skill catalog regions, or any builder-projected skill block.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex pattern transfer may produce or refine a skill only with std_annex_distillation provenance (annex_slug, pattern_id, source_locus) plus provenance.status of annex_derived recorded on the skill row and a back-pointer in the source annex notes.
known_gaps: Many active skills still lack the skill_type axis from std_skill_types.json; the backfill pass is in progress and tracked by skill_compression_debt rows.
Compression-passport and agent_surface coverage is uneven across the 200+ active skills.

type_plane_rows.title: Axiom Candidates
type_plane_rows.source_authority: obsidian/<phase_dir>/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json — typed candidate clauses that sit above pri_* rows but are not active doctrine until promoted.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author a complete axiom_candidate row with formal_clause, dense_clause, all five compression_expansion_bands (tiny, flag, card, context, deep), at least one teleological_deliverable with desired_capability/target_surface/proof_hint, evidence_refs, dependency_neighborhood_evidence, russian_doll_exemplar_chain spanning raw paragraph through axiom row, violation_predicates, and activation_packet_behavior. authority_posture must say candidate_not_active_doctrine unless an explicit promotion receipt is cited.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the axiom_candidates option surface (flag, card, tape) before opening the family ledger JSON; expect candidate posture to remain explicit even when surfaced as runtime pressure in entry packets.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project AxiomCandidate entities with related_principles edges and teleology_refs to tel_* nodes; row status (candidate, contested, promoted, rejected, superseded) is preserved on the projection.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Candidate axioms are typed proposals, not live doctrine; cadence requires explicit disposition events when surfaced by failure, repeated operator correction, or active downstream consumers (see std_system_axiom_candidate.curation_cadence_contract).
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/raw_seed_apply_loop.py link-teleology --spec <spec> --commit --rationale <reason>; promotion to a pri_* row goes through raw_seed principles curation, not direct hand-mint from the axiom ledger.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: External annex content may motivate a candidate but cannot become an axiom by import. The operator-voice authority gate, full row schema, and the russian_doll_exemplar_chain anchored in raw paragraph or agent synthesis still apply.
known_gaps: candidate_to_runtime_packet wiring into the entry/context-pack assembly path is partially complete; std_system_axiom_candidate.follow_on_implementation_debt names the remaining work.
Most candidates carry only the legacy teleology profile and have not yet adopted shared teleology_refs.

type_plane_rows.title: Shared Desire / Teleology Nodes
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/teleology_nodes.json — first-class shared desires that several principles, anti-principles, axiom candidates, and anti-axioms can reference.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author a complete tel_* node with id, title, desire_statement, end_state, agent_experience, operator_experience, deliverables (each with deliverable_id/status/desired_capability/target_surface/proof_hint), success_signals, non_goals, and evidence_refs. Teleology nodes compress the wanted world; many positive and anti commitments should share a single node.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Browse the teleologies option surface, then traverse principles_by_teleology and axioms_by_teleology to see which positive commitments and anti-profiles share the desire. Anti rows must reuse the parent's teleology_refs; they cannot define separate desires.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Teleology entities with incoming teleology_refs edges from principles, axiom_candidates, anti-principles, and anti-axioms; the compression rule (teleologies < positive/anti pairs < positive + negative rows) is preserved on the projection.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Teleology nodes are stable shared-desire compressions; mutation must keep teleologies fewer than the positive/anti pairs they explain. Adding a new node requires an authored desire body and at least one teleology_ref-bearing consumer.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/raw_seed_apply_loop.py link-teleology --spec <spec> --commit --rationale <reason>; do not duplicate teleology bodies row-by-row in principles or axiom_candidates, and do not hand-edit teleology_nodes.json.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex content cannot become a teleology by import; shared desire is operator-grounded, expressed in raw seed, and authored through the apply lane after sufficient cross-row pressure.
known_gaps: Most pri_* and axiom_candidate_* rows still lack teleology_refs or authored teleology profiles; cap_principle_teleology_navigation_population owns the population work.
Only six principles and three axiom candidates currently link via teleology_refs; coverage is below the compression_rule's intended ratio.

type_plane_rows.title: Anti-Principles
type_plane_rows.source_authority: anti_principle bodies attached to pri_* rows in obsidian/<phase_dir>/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json; teleology_refs must equal the parent principle's teleology_refs exactly.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author an anti_principle that shares its parent principle's teleology_refs verbatim. Required fields: id, title, failure_statement, teleology_refs, failure_modes, detection_signals. Forbidden fields: teleology, desire_statement, end_state, agent_experience, operator_experience, deliverables, success_signals, non_goals, evidence_refs. Optional routed-profile fields (failure_attractor, constitutional_risk, recovery_protocol, route_when_detected, route_surfaces, route_commands, boundary_conditions, failure_evidence_refs) describe how the shared desire fails, not a new desire.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Browse anti_principles from a chosen teleology or principle; treat anti rows as failure-attractor maps around the same shared desire, not as independent principles. The negative-space map exists to make the positive principle's failure mode legible.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project AntiPrinciple entities with paired-with edges to the parent principle and shares_teleology_with edges to the same tel_* nodes.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Anti-principle profiles drift when the parent principle's failure modes evolve or when a routed profile gains attractor/recovery detail; refresh on principle revisions and operator-correction-driven curation cycles.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/raw_seed_apply_loop.py link-teleology --spec <spec> --commit --rationale <reason>; same lane as the parent principle, never hand-edited as standalone JSON.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex distillation may surface failure attractors, but the anti_principle row must still carry the parent principle's teleology_refs verbatim and must not introduce independent goals or evidence_refs.
known_gaps: Routed-profile fields (failure_attractor, constitutional_risk, recovery_protocol, route_commands) are populated on only a small subset of anti_principles.
Many pri_* rows still lack a paired anti_principle, so the negative-space map is incomplete.

type_plane_rows.title: Anti-Axioms
type_plane_rows.source_authority: anti_axiom bodies attached to axiom_candidate rows in obsidian/<phase_dir>/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json; teleology_refs must equal the parent axiom_candidate's teleology_refs exactly.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author an anti_axiom that shares its parent axiom_candidate's teleology_refs verbatim. Required fields: id, title, failure_statement, teleology_refs, failure_modes, detection_signals. Forbidden fields mirror anti_principles: no teleology body or desire fields. Routed-profile fields describe how the shared constitutional desire fails, not a new desire.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Browse anti_axioms from a chosen teleology or axiom_candidate; anti rows describe constitutional failure attractors around the same shared desire and inherit candidate posture from the parent row.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project AntiAxiom entities with paired-with edges to the parent axiom_candidate and shares_teleology_with edges to tel_* nodes; the parent's candidate status is preserved on the projection.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Anti-axiom profiles drift when the parent axiom_candidate is revised or when a routed profile gains constitutional_risk/recovery_protocol detail; refresh on axiom revisions and on disposition events under std_system_axiom_candidate.curation_cadence_contract.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/raw_seed_apply_loop.py link-teleology --spec <spec> --commit --rationale <reason>; same lane as the parent axiom_candidate.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex distillation may surface failure attractors, but the anti_axiom row must carry the parent axiom_candidate's teleology_refs verbatim and must not redefine the desire or assert active doctrine.
known_gaps: anti_axiom coverage is sparse (four rows authored against fourteen axiom_candidates); routed-profile fields populated only on the most pressure-bearing rows.
Several axiom_candidates have no anti_axiom yet, so the constitutional failure-attractor map is partial.

type_plane_rows.title: Task Ledger WorkItems
type_plane_rows.source_authority: state/task_ledger/events.jsonl as canonical append-only event log; state/task_ledger/ledger.json and state/task_ledger/views/*.json are projections of those events.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author or mutate WorkItem rows through the task_ledger_apply lane (quick-capture, claim, refine, sign-off, rebuild). Each row carries id, slug, title, statement, status, rank with rank_history justification, compression_passport, created_at, updated_at, author. Hard prerequisite edges go in depends_on; broad context goes in dependencies; sign-off uses std_task_sign_off.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the task_ledger option surface (cluster_flag, then flag, then card) and the views directory; never grep ledger.json for routing. The Task Ledger is the durable next-action surface for any agent — Type A or Type B — and outlives chat-ephemeral todos.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project WorkItem entities with depends_on and blocks edges, claim/sign_off lifecycle, rank with rank_history, and tags; mutation routes back to the apply lane and the event log, never atlas state.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Drift-sensitive: views are derived from events.jsonl plus ledger.json and must be rebuilt after every apply. Re-rank without an appended rank_history justification breaks the audit chain and is rejected at validation.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/task_ledger_apply.py {quick-capture,claim,refine,sign-off,...}; append authority first. Add --rebuild only when projection/card visibility is required and uncontended; otherwise settle with drain-deferred-rebuilds or generated_state_drainer for task_ledger_projection. Never hand-edit ledger.json, events.jsonl, or generated views.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex pattern transfer may motivate a WorkItem but the cap row must carry its own evidence_refs and route through quick-capture; annex provenance lives in tags or notes, not as authority.
known_gaps: Many caps still carry only a default compression_passport; flag/card density varies across the 1000+ WorkItem population.
Hard depends_on coverage is partial; broad dependencies often carry routing weight that depends_on should bear, leaving schedulability projections under-typed.

type_plane_rows.title: Task Ledger Cap Cards
type_plane_rows.source_authority: state/task_ledger/events.jsonl as canonical append-only event log; cap cards render state/task_ledger/ledger.json and state/task_ledger/views/*.json projections only.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Cap renderer cards may expose guarded Task Ledger actions, but mutation still goes through task_ledger_apply.py and rebuilt projections.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the task_ledger option surface, then render the selected cap through the renderer passport so state, dependencies, receipts, blockers, and next safe mutation are explicit.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project WorkItem entities; task_ledger_caps is the renderer profile for cap cards, not a separate authority plane.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Cap cards are fresh only when task_ledger_apply.py validate --allow-warnings and rebuilt views agree with events.jsonl.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/task_ledger_apply.py {quick-capture,claim,refine,sign-off,...}; append authority first. Add --rebuild only when projection/card visibility is required and uncontended; otherwise settle with drain-deferred-rebuilds or generated_state_drainer for task_ledger_projection. Never hand-edit generated Task Ledger projections.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex-derived work may become a cap only through Task Ledger capture with evidence refs and tags; renderer cards do not import annex authority directly.
known_gaps: Task Ledger cap cards still need a dedicated Station renderer that consumes renderer_passports instead of inferring card layout ad hoc.

type_plane_rows.title: Routes and Option Surfaces
type_plane_rows.source_authority: system/lib/kind_atlas.py, system/lib/standard_option_surface.py, and governing route/navigation standards; generated command output is projection only.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Add or revise route and option-surface behavior in the owning standard or Python adapter, then validate through kernel output; do not create parallel trigger surfaces.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Start from --entry, then --kind-atlas, then --option-surface <kind_id>; route cards must name surface role, selectable rows, evidence commands, omission receipts, and control replacement.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: Kind Atlas is the rung-0 projection for artifact-kind selection. It names supported option surfaces but does not own source authority for the rows it links to.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Route/currentness checks come from kernel entry/context-pack, kind-atlas rows, and generated-artifact-surface adapters.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit governing standards and owning adapter modules, then run focused kernel route tests. Generated output packets are never hand-edited.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex navigation ideas may become local route patterns only after landing in std_kind_atlas, std_navigation_contract, a paper module, or an owning adapter with tests.
known_gaps: Generic route cards still compose several adapters; renderer passports provide the common frontend contract until a route-specific renderer standard exists.

type_plane_rows.title: Frontend Views
type_plane_rows.source_authority: system/server/ui/src plus generated frontend navigation graph and UI receipts; generated graph rows are projection, not UI source authority.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Frontend views are implemented in system/server/ui/src under view, receipt, and station aesthetic standards; generated graph/index artifacts are refreshed through their owning tools.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use frontend_views and frontend_components option surfaces before opening TSX source; renderer cards must expose view contract, fact bindings, decision contract, fixtures, density bands, and receipts.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project frontend views and view/component edges, but mutations route back to UI source and receipt builders.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: View graph, component index, and UI receipt checks must be refreshed after view or navigation changes.

cluster_flag.purpose: Cluster the generated frontend navigation graph by shell/station group so agents can choose a view family before opening row cards.

flag.purpose: Expose one frontend view row with route, purpose, capture, source component, visual-memory refs, currentness, and card drilldown.

card.purpose: Bind a selected view to source evidence, outbound/inbound navigation, capture contract, visual memory, visual settlement, and omission receipts.

band:cluster_flag.source: state/frontend_navigation/navigation_graph.json plus view observation and settlement indexes

band:card.source: navigation graph, station_views.json capture rows, visual memory index, and visual settlement index

source_authority.projection_boundary: Generated graph, visual memory, and visual settlement are browse projections; TS/TSX source and receipt builders remain mutation authority.

currentness_policy.rule: Run the view graph check after route, surface, overlay, station view, or capture edits; do not hand-edit generated frontend navigation or visual-memory projections.

dependency_neighborhood_policy.rule: Card rows expose direct route edges, source component evidence, capture contracts, and visual-memory refs; wider graph/component closures downshift to counts and drilldown commands.

type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit system/server/ui/src and run frontend/UI receipt checks; do not hand-edit state/frontend_navigation projections.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: External UI/wiki patterns may inform view shape, but local renderer behavior must cite the view contract, UI receipt standard, and source component path.
known_gaps: The Station standards/wiki lens has not yet consumed renderer_passports directly.

type_plane_rows.title: Operator Action Receipts
type_plane_rows.source_authority: state/operator_bridge/hud_actions.jsonl remains the durable historical receipt log for archived HUD/operator actions; tools/meta/observability/retired_launchd/RETIRED.md owns the retirement receipt. Live prompt-run observability now routes through browser-free transcript capture from local Claude Code / Codex transcripts.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Operator controls that make semantic claims must classify claim/proof/body fields. Proof-governed actions preserve required proof fields or mark proof_status=missing_required_fields and receipt_incomplete=true; raw body/private/debug payloads stay out of durable metadata.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: For archived HUD/operator actions, inspect hud_actions.jsonl rows, std_operator_action_receipt, and the retirement receipt before trusting a copied/exported artifact's completeness claim. For current prompt-run evidence, use prompt_shelf_transcript_capture.py scan/write and prompt_shelf_runs_index.py summary/coverage.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project historical operator action receipt posture and current transcript-capture run metadata, but source authority remains the durable jsonl log plus the receipt standard for archived actions and prompt-shelf raw events/run notes for current prompt runs.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Archived HUD action health is historical after the 2026-06-13 retirement; do not use CDP/HUD observer checks as live currentness proof. Current prompt-run capture is current only after transcript-capture scan/write and prompt-shelf run summary/coverage readback.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit std_operator_action_receipt for archived operator-action receipt semantics, or prompt_shelf_transcript_capture.py / prompt_shelf_capture_writer.py for current prompt-run observability; do not hand-edit hud_actions.jsonl, raw_events, run notes, or broaden durable receipt payloads.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: External logging or trace-context patterns may shape claim/proof/body vocabulary only after landing in std_operator_action_receipt and preserving the metadata-only privacy policy.
known_gaps: No dedicated option-surface adapter exists yet for browsing historic operator action receipts; current prompt-run observability is covered by transcript capture and the prompt-shelf runs index.

type_plane_rows.title: Public Plectis Exports

compression_passport.atom: Type-plane bridge from public Plectis export questions into product doctrine, entry-lattice depth, behavior proof, and disclosure-bound projection checks.
compression_passport.when_to_open: Open when a task asks how public Plectis exports, public projection files, dissemination boundaries, or Plectis export readiness should route through standards, paper modules, and first-screen behavior proof.
compression_passport.when_not_to_open: Do not open as release approval, source truth, provider proof, candidate-axiom authority, or a substitute for the renderer/publication receipt owned by dissemination builders.
landmines: Public export navigation is not publication approval.
Generated public files are not internal source authority.
Behavior proof, paper-module depth, and claim-boundary accounting must be visible before export file inspection.
sufficiency_claims: Names the Plectis paper-module depth order, supporting lattice depth, entry-depth contract, validation probes, and source/projection boundaries.
Routes back to std_microcosm and microcosm_public_export_type_plane before generated public export files.

type_plane_rows.source_authority: microcosm-substrate/, publication_manifest.yaml, self-indexing-cognitive-substrate/ archaeology, and dissemination builders; public exports are projections over internal source authority and public Plectis owner surfaces while Microcosm remains a compatibility label.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Public Plectis exports require redaction, synthetic fixture, rights, and projection receipts before publication; internal authority remains upstream.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use standards-first entry, then the Plectis local compatibility first-screen sequence (`microcosm hello <project>` for the non-writing map, `microcosm tour --card <project>` for behavior proof, and `microcosm first-screen <project>` for JSON route shape), then microcosm_substrate product doctrine, microcosm_entry_lattice route depth, microcosm_public_export_type_plane bridge guidance, microcosm_runtime_organ_atlas source-loci depth, paper_module_coverage_metabolism, paper_module_entry_projection_integrity, and dissemination/public constellation context before opening public export files; the legacy system_microcosm option surface is a Laboratory/control-view compatibility route, not the public Plectis definition.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project public export candidates only as public/dissemination leaves with disclosure and provenance posture.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Public exports are current only when the publication manifest, renderer output, receipt bundle, and Plectis first-screen/authority/workingness contracts agree with the selected internal authority snapshot.

entry_depth_contract.export_depth_rule: Open generated public export files only after behavior proof, paper-module depth, and claim-boundary accounting are visible.

type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Use dissemination/public projection builders and manifests; never make public HTML, Markdown, or demos the source of internal truth.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: External LLM-wiki or HTML export patterns may shape packaging only after redaction/provenance gates prove no private authority leakage.

type_plane_rows.title: Derived Facts / State Axes
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/facts/fact_registry.json — provider registry declaring how each fact is derived; codex/hologram/facts/ledger.json, codex/hologram/facts/audit.json, and codex/hologram/facts/navigation_cache.json are the generated current values and must not be hand-edited.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Extend the provider registry (FactProvider rows) when a new derived fact is needed; bind paper-module current-state claims through std_derived_fact.types.FactAssertion. Never write generated ledger, audit, or navigation_cache state directly. Provider rows declare extraction method, source_path or callable, semantic tags, and navigation hints.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the facts option surface (cluster_flag, then flag, then card) or the navigation_cache for fact lookup; treat fact rows as runtime state projections, not authored doctrine. State adjectives ('is X dirty/clean/pending/ready/active?') route through facts; the same questions in prose are drift-prone.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Fact entities with provider_ref edges, owner_surface anchors, and tags; mutation routes back to fact_registry.json or the derived-fact builder.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Generated values are rebuilt by build_fact_hologram.py from the provider registry plus current repo state. Drift sensitivity is high: a missing provider run leaves audited paper-module claims pinned to stale values.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Edit codex/doctrine/facts/fact_registry.json (provider rows), then rerun ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/build_fact_hologram.py to refresh generated state; never hand-edit codex/hologram/facts/*.json.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex content may suggest a fact provider shape, but the local provider row must still satisfy std_derived_fact.types.FactProvider and route back to a real source_path or callable in this repo.
known_gaps: Many paper modules carry prose claims not yet bound to FactAssertion rows; the live-vs-prose drift is the failure mode this standard exists to close.
Provider coverage for state-axis questions such as workitem schedulability and navigation route freshness is partial; new providers are added on-demand.

type_plane_rows.title: Imaginations
type_plane_rows.source_authority: codex/doctrine/imaginations/<id>.md as authored counterfactual scenes; codex/doctrine/imaginations/_index.json is generated and must not be hand-edited.
type_plane_rows.write_contract: Author an imagination markdown body with the std_imagination.json::required_frontmatter_fields and a vivid present-tense scene. An imagination renders 'what would become possible if the future-state were already true' — it is not a principle (rule), axiom_candidate (compressed rule), standard (contract), paper_module (current-state explanation), pattern_deliverable (backlog row), or roadmap commitment. Hedges live in if_it_were_true_frame, never in body sentences.
type_plane_rows.read_contract: Use the imaginations option surface and the imaginations index; treat counterfactual scenes as affordance previews that reveal which substrate or surface is needed to make the future-state real, not as predictions or aspirational prose.
type_plane_rows.atlas_projection_policy: System Atlas may project Imagination entities with migrated_from edges back to source axiom_candidate teleological_deliverables, voice_anchor edges to raw-seed paragraphs, and closest_existing_substrate edges into current substrate.
type_plane_rows.currentness_policy: Imaginations are stable counterfactual scenes; lifecycle status (active, retired_superseded, etc.) and voice_anchor.resolution.checked govern when a scene must be rerun against current substrate after substrate drift.
type_plane_rows.mutation_lane: Author or revise codex/doctrine/imaginations/<id>.md, then run ./repo-python tools/meta/factory/build_imagination_index.py to refresh the generated index; field-set additions or enum changes require a v2 schema bump on std_imagination.
type_plane_rows.annex_adaptation_policy: Annex content may motivate a new imagination, but the scene must be authored locally with voice_anchor and closest_existing_substrate grounded in this repo, not in the annex source.
known_gaps: Many axiom_candidate teleological_deliverables[] rows have not yet been migrated to imaginations even when std_system_axiom_candidate.teleological_deliverables_migration would mark migration_action standalone_new_imagination or grouped_into_new_imagination.
Imagination coverage of frontend, dispatch, and bridge subsystem affordances is partial.

annex_logic_contract.rule: Use annexes for actual source-backed logic, not vibes. A usable annex transfer names the upstream source_locus, the local target type, the target governing standard, the adoption lane, the local acceptance test, and the currentness check.

known_annex_precedents.logic: Navigation files stay separate from full evidence stores.
known_annex_precedents.local_use: Keep standard/type rows thin and route to evidence bodies instead of embedding them.

known_annex_precedents.logic: Structure is selected before page/content retrieval.
known_annex_precedents.local_use: Type plane selects artifact kind and native band before opening artifact bodies.

known_annex_precedents.logic: Graph repairs are first-class suggestion objects with id, source, target, status, score, explanation, and feedback.
known_annex_precedents.local_use: Navigation/atlas repair candidates should become typed caps or suggestion rows, not prose-only observations.
Type-plane changes and annex adaptations need append-only ledger/cap receipts.

forbidden: Citing an annex pattern without source_locus when moving beyond proposal.
Letting an annex pattern override a local standard.
Copying an upstream architecture when ai_workflow already has a stronger native owner.
Treating paper modules or atlas rows as the landing authority for annex-derived rules.

operator_pressure.statement: The emerging LLM-wiki/HTML-wiki pattern should be assimilated into ai_workflow through the stronger native primitive: standards and JSON/JSWN-style contracts as the grammar that makes artifact classes renderable, queryable, validatable, and safely exportable.

executable_wiki_projection_contract.rule: A standard is executable wiki grammar for its artifact type. It defines source authority, write contract, read contract, projection refs, density bands, drilldown commands, validation probes, public/private policy, and receipt expectations before any HTML, Markdown, Obsidian, command-output, or React page projection is trusted.
external_pattern_taken: raw sources stay separate from compiled knowledge surfaces
agents maintain durable interlinked pages instead of answer-only chat
static HTML or graph exports make the surface browsable
lint/maintain/report loops keep the compiled layer alive
native_generalization: Standards, not pages, own artifact-class grammar.
JSON/JSWN-style authority contracts decide machine shape; Markdown and HTML are projections.
Every renderer starts from artifact kind and governing standard, not from a generic document template.
Every low-band page carries omission receipts and drilldown commands.
Every public export routes through public-safety and provenance gates before publication.
Projection trust comes from source manifest, freshness, validation, and receipts, not from page existence.

passport_derivation_rule.rule: Every type_plane_rows entry produces a renderer passport. artifact_class_renderer_profiles are renderer_job/governing_refs overrides for the matching artifact_kind; their absence does not gate passport generation.
passport_derivation_rule.why: A type-plane row already declares governing_standard_refs, source_authority, option_surface_command, validation_probe, mutation_lane, and projection_refs — exactly the contract a renderer passport needs. Gating passports on a separately-maintained profile list duplicated the registration step, left fifteen of nineteen type-plane rows passport-less, and made the renderer_passports option surface an under-coverage signal instead of a complete alignment surface.
passport_derivation_rule.renderer_job_fallback: When no explicit profile names a renderer_job, the builder derives one from the type_plane_row title plus the standard renderer contract. Authoring an artifact_class_renderer_profiles entry overrides this fallback when bespoke renderer language is needed.
passport_derivation_rule.single_source_invariant: The standards type plane is the single drop point for adding a renderable artifact kind. Adding a type_plane_rows entry registers it everywhere downstream — kind atlas, option surfaces, system atlas projections, and the renderer passport projection — without a separate profile registration step.

artifact_class_renderer_profiles.renderer_job: Show purpose, source authority, read/write contracts, validation probes, type-plane links, related caps, and renderer passport.
Show compressed subsystem claim, trust posture, dependencies, code loci, gaps, route coverage, and refresh command.
Show state, rank, dependencies, satisfaction contract, exact surfaces, blockers, execution receipts, and next safe mutation.
Show selectable rows, evidence commands, omission receipts, surface role, and control replacement.
Show view contract, fact bindings, decision contract, density bands, fixtures, receipt policy, and render receipts.
Show action identity, semantic claim, required proof fields, proof_status, missing proof, metadata-only policy, health projection, and source receipt log.
Show source authority, redaction/synthetic fixture policy, portability gate, rights posture, and publication boundary.

forbidden: Treating HTML, Markdown, Obsidian, or a React lens as source authority.
Rendering an artifact class without naming its governing standard and source authority.
Generic document templates that erase artifact-class semantics.
Public wiki export without public-safety, redaction, and provenance gates.
Replacing the standards type plane with a separate wiki-specific ontology.

queryable_projection_contract.source_change_refresh_rule: Any change to type_plane_rows, required_type_row_fields, executable_wiki_projection_contract, or queryable_projection_contract must refresh/check the type-plane graph, renderer passports, idea-microcosm projection, and agent bootstrap projection before closeout.
question_shapes: What standard owns this artifact type?
What command lets an agent browse this type before source open?
What source authority writes this row?
Which annex patterns can improve it?
What caps or gaps block it?
forbidden: Manual edits to generated type-plane state.
Projection rows that omit source authority or governing standard.
Source bodies in low-band browse output.

validation_rules: Every type row must name at least one governing standard and a source authority.
Every type row must distinguish write_contract from read_contract.
Every type row must name either a working option_surface_command or a known_gaps entry explaining the missing adapter.
Projection refs cannot be the only authority for a type row.
Annex adaptation must point to the target type id and target governing standard before local adoption is claimed.
Queryable projection surfaces remain generated state with source manifests and freshness status.
Paper modules may describe subsystem shape, but standards remain the landing authority for write/read/navigation contracts.

HTML, Markdown, Obsidian, command-output, and React wiki surfaces are projections; a valid renderer must route through the governing standard, source authority, drilldown commands, validation probes, and receipt policy.
The renderer passport projection covers every type_plane_rows entry; profiles override the default renderer_job but never gate passport generation.
non_goals: Do not replace native standards with one universal schema.
Do not make the System Atlas authoritative over the standards it projects.
Do not make paper modules the home for standards-grade navigation rules.
Do not implement the generated queryable database in this standard file.
Do not turn the executable-wiki framing into a parallel wiki ontology separate from the standards type plane.

Do not gate renderer passport coverage on a separately-maintained profile list when type_plane_rows already declare governing standard, source authority, validation probes, projection refs, and mutation lane.
